note: Verbatim phrases of the locked narration, in spoken order, split at the script's break tags, sentence boundaries, and the TTS's real intra-sentence pauses (verified against silencedetect so voiced-segment counts match). Caption text must never be rewritten, summarized, or reordered. Block windows are MEASURED onsets (cross-correlation of each per-step mp3 against the joined master, corr=1.0 for all 12 blocks): the joiner used ~0.365s inter-block gaps, not 0.4s, so formula windows drift up to -0.44s by s12. Final end = ffprobe of the master (167.158s).

phrases.text: Open Agent Console from the Service Desk group in the sidebar.
One screen, three panes.
The queue on the left,
the live conversation in the middle,
the customer's details on the right.
No tab-hopping while someone waits on you.
Up in the header, your presence.
It reads Offline right now, and it can be Available, Away, Busy, or Offline.
This is how the desk knows whether you're ready for a contact.
Leave it where your shift set it; we don't touch it here.
Look at the top of the queue.
Three numbers give you the load at a glance:
how many are waiting, the average wait, and how many are flagged high priority.
All zero now.
A calm board.
There's a search box over the queue.
Type a name, a phone number, a plan, or the reason for the handoff, and the list narrows to it.
On a busy shift, that's how you pull one contact out of a crowd.
Three buttons scope the queue.
Mine is yours.
Unassigned is up for grabs.
Team is the whole group's board.
Right now it's set to Team, so you're seeing everything, not only your own.
Below that, the channels.
All,
then Voice,
S-M-S, and Web.
Those last three are the ways a customer reaches you, and All shows them together.
Remember the three:
voice, text,
and web chat.
Open the sort control.
You can work the queue longest wait first, priority first, or most recent first.
Longest wait first is the fair choice;
it clears whoever has been holding the longest.
Close it without changing anything.
Under the filters, the queue itself.
It says no matching handoffs, because nothing is waiting.
When a contact comes in, its row shows up right here for you to pick up.
The middle is empty too.
Select a handoff from the queue, it says, to open its console.
That's the cue.
You click a waiting row, and the conversation loads into this space.
And on the right, the Customer panel.
No conversation selected, for now.
Once you open one, this is where the customer's context sits, so you're never guessing who you're helping.
So why is it all empty?
The queue fills when the AI Agent hands a conversation to a person.
Those escalations land right here, in the Handoff Queue.
The agent side of that is a later lesson; for now, know this is where a handed-off customer waits for you.
One screen, three panes.
The queue you scope by who owns it and by channel,
the console that opens the conversation,
and the customer's context beside it.
Your presence lives in the header, and you leave it alone unless your shift changes.
That's the Agent Console.
